title: Cannot drop `CHECK CONSTRAINT` if `tidb_enable_check_constraint` is `OFF`.
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
set global tidb_enable_check_constraint = 'ON';
CREATE TABLE t1 (CHECK (c1 <> c2), c1 INT CHECK (c1 > 10), c2 INT CONSTRAINT c2_positive_for_t1 CHECK (c2 > 0));
set global tidb_enable_check_constraint = 'OFF';
alter table t1 drop check c2_positive_for_t1;
```

It'll return a warning and don't actually drop the check constraint. 

To avoid X-Y problem, let me describe why we might need to drop without enabling `tidb_enable_check_constraint`:

If a cluster creates constraints with the same name in a single database before https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/48062 is introduced, and upgrade to a newer version with https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/48062, it will fail to backup/restore. The restore will produce an error: `Duplicate check constraint name ...`.

I have two proposal / options for this issue:

1. Modify the DDL to allow drop check even when `tidb_enable_check_constraint` is off.
2. Modify the BR to automatically clean up the check constraints when `tidb_enable_check_constraint` is off. It sounds weird but the behavior is the same with `CREATE TABLE` DDL SQL.

I personally prefer the first one, but not sure.

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

The constraint is dropped.

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

It's not dropped and a warning is returned.

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
poc: CREATE TABLE t1 (CHECK (c1 <> c2), c1 INT CHECK (c1 > 10), c2 INT CONSTRAINT c2_positive_for_t1 CHECK (c2 > 0));
alter table t1 drop check c2_positive_for_t1;
key_bug_features: Dropping check constraint with feature disabled
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, age INT CHECK (age >= 0), salary DECIMAL(10,2) CHECK (salary > 0));
ALTER TABLE t2 DROP CHECK age >= 0;
INSERT INTO t2 (id, name, age, salary) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 30, 50000.00);
